channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as U.S. farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded:
Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.

(Image credit: Frank Morris)
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: National Mall is propaganda battlefield for Trump and his critics
item.description: Banners bearing the face of President Trump vie with satiric statues and protest posters in a propaganda battle playing out in and around the National Mall.
item.content:encoded: Banners bearing the face of President Trump vie with satiric statues and protest posters in a propaganda battle playing out in and around the National Mall.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: A shelter village provides a bridge to permanent housing
item.description: Shelter villages offer temporary and private places for the unhoused to sleep and store belongings. One of the newest, The Bridge, opened recently in central Illinois.
item.content:encoded: Shelter villages offer temporary and private places for the unhoused to sleep and store belongings. One of the newest, The Bridge, opened recently in central Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bollinger

item.title: Travelers are facing the longest TSA wait times in history
item.description: Wait times are exceeding four hours at some major airports, leading TSA officers to call out at rates of 40 to 50%, according to TSA Deputy Administrator Ha Nguyen McNeill.
item.content:encoded:
Wait times are exceeding four hours at some major airports, leading TSA officers to call out at rates of 40 to 50%, according to TSA Deputy Administrator Ha Nguyen McNeill.

(Image credit: Ryan Murphy)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in social media harms trial
item.description: The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Savannah Guthrie's first interview since mother Nancy vanished: 'I imagine her terror'
item.description: Nearly two months after Nancy Guthrie disappeared, her daughter Savannah discusses the toll on her family in an emotional interview with her Today show colleague Hoda Kotb.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly two months after Nancy Guthrie disappeared, her daughter Savannah discusses the toll on her family in an emotional interview with her Today show colleague Hoda Kotb.

(Image credit: Rebecca Noble)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: A 92-year-old judge will take on the Maduro case. What do we know about him?
item.description: Former Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro heads to court again this week. The judge overseeing this case is longtime federal Judge Alvin Hellerstein. At 92 years old, Hellerstein is older than the average age of a federal judge by more than 20 years.
item.content:encoded:
Former Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro heads to court again this week. The judge overseeing this case is longtime federal Judge Alvin Hellerstein. At 92 years old, Hellerstein is older than the average age of a federal judge by more than 20 years.

(Image credit: Jane Rosenberg)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions
item.description: Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes war reparations and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes war reparations and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: They gave her business a lifeline, then froze all her money
item.description: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.content:encoded: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Your data is everywhere. The government is buying it without a warrant
item.description: Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.
item.content:encoded:
Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block
